$description: IBM Carbon Design System flavor — a full DTCG override of the webtheme platform default capturing Carbon's core token roles: #0f62fe interactive blue, 0 radius, compact density, and IBM Plex type scale. Transcribed from Carbon v11's light theme (White theme) token set. Values are authored in oklch where the source hex has a known perceptual equivalent; the IBM blue accent is authored as hex because #0f62fe is the carbon-exact value the workbench spec names. See the paired gap list report for what these roles CAN and CANNOT reach in a rendered FUI component. Note: traitDefaults radius=none plus the zero-radius override together express Carbon's signature no-radius shape.

color.$description: Carbon v11 light theme (White) color roles — the interactive, layer, and text tokens a component reads. Carbon ships a layered model ($layer-01 through $layer-04, $layer-accent-*) with no named accent that cleanly maps to a single hue-seed; $interactive maps here as the closest accent role.

accent.$description: Carbon $interactive — IBM Blue 60, the primary interactive colour. Authored as hex to preserve the carbon-exact value the workbench spec names.

radius.$description: Carbon's no-radius shape — every interactive element is sharp-cornered (radius 0). base=0 cascades to every derived step; md and lg are explicit zeroes so the DTCG bridge rows for radius.md and radius.lg each resolve to 0 and visibly override the platform default (0.5rem / 1rem).

surface.$description: Carbon neutral surface roles — border and muted-surface anchors from the Gray scale.
